rows.content_text: Hoo Sheau Peng J:
rows.html_raw: Hoo Sheau Peng J:

rows.content_text: Introduction
rows.html_raw: Introduction

rows.content_text:
At the height of the Covid-19 pandemic, the defendant engaged the plaintiff to produce and deliver nitrile powder-free gloves under a supply agreement and three purchase orders. There were delays in the deliveries.

---table---
Smart Glove International Pte Ltd
---end-table---


---table---
Full Support Healthcare Ltd
---end-table---


---table---
Full Support Healthcare Ltd
---end-table---


---table---
Smart Glove International Pte Ltd
---end-table---


---table---
This judgment is subject to final editorial corrections approved by the court and/or redaction pursuant to the publisher’s duty in compliance with the law, for publication in LawNet and/or the Singapore Law Reports.
---end-table---
rows.html_raw: 1 At the height of the Covid-19 pandemic, the defendant engaged the plaintiff to produce and deliver nitrile powder-free gloves under a supply agreement and three purchase orders. There were delays in the deliveries.

rows.content_text: The defendant argues, as part of both its defence and its counterclaim, that these delays, among other events, constituted repudiatory breaches by the plaintiff, which it accepted through an email sent on 20 April 2021. It seeks reliefs, including damages and the refund of certain monies paid to the plaintiff (which it argues are refundable advance payments).
rows.html_raw: 2 The defendant argues, as part of both its defence and its counterclaim, that these delays, among other events, constituted repudiatory breaches by the plaintiff, which it accepted through an email sent on 20 April 2021. It seeks reliefs, including damages and the refund of certain monies paid to the plaintiff (which it argues are refundable advance payments).

rows.content_text: On the other hand, the plaintiff argues, in its claim and its defence to the defendant’s counterclaim, that it was the defendant who was in breach of its obligations by refusing to continue performing its obligations after the email was sent. It asks for reliefs, including damages and specific performance. Additionally, the plaintiff also seeks to retain all monies received from the defendant (arguing that they are forfeitable deposits).
rows.html_raw: 3 On the other hand, the plaintiff argues, in its claim and its defence to the defendant’s counterclaim, that it was the defendant who was in breach of its obligations by refusing to continue performing its obligations after the email was sent. It asks for reliefs, including damages and specific performance. Additionally, the plaintiff also seeks to retain all monies received from the defendant (arguing that they are forfeitable deposits).

rows.content_text: Having heard the parties and considered the evidence, I find that both parties were in breach of the contracts. Further, I find that the monies paid in advance by the defendant to the plaintiff are refundable advance payments. These are my reasons.
rows.html_raw: 4 Having heard the parties and considered the evidence, I find that both parties were in breach of the contracts. Further, I find that the monies paid in advance by the defendant to the plaintiff are refundable advance payments. These are my reasons.

rows.content_text: Facts
rows.html_raw: Facts

rows.content_text: The parties
rows.html_raw: The parties

rows.content_text: The plaintiff, Smart Glove International Pte Ltd (“SGI”), is a Singapore-incorporated company in the business of selling and distributing medical equipment, including gloves. SGI is wholly owned by Smart Glove Holdings Sdn Bhd (“SGH”), a company incorporated in Malaysia. 1 Foot Note 1 × Plaintiff’s Closing Submissions (“PCS”) at para 8. SGH is also the ultimate holding company of a group of companies, including Smart Glove Corporation Sdn Bhd (“SGC”), GX Corporation Sdn Bhd (“GX”), Platinum Glove Industries Sdn Bhd (“PGI”), and Sigma Glove Industries Sdn Bhd (“Sigma”). 2 Foot Note 2 × PCS at para 9. In this judgment, I will refer to SGH and its subsidiary companies collectively as the “Smart Glove Group” or “SGG”.
rows.html_raw:
5 The plaintiff, Smart Glove International Pte Ltd (“SGI”), is a Singapore-incorporated company in the business of selling and distributing medical equipment, including gloves. SGI is wholly owned by Smart Glove Holdings Sdn Bhd (“SGH”), a company incorporated in Malaysia.
Foot Note 1
 Plaintiff’s Closing Submissions (“PCS”) at para 8.
SGH is also the ultimate holding company of a group of companies, including Smart Glove Corporation Sdn Bhd (“SGC”), GX Corporation Sdn Bhd (“GX”), Platinum Glove Industries Sdn Bhd (“PGI”), and Sigma Glove Industries Sdn Bhd (“Sigma”).
Foot Note 2
 PCS at para 9.
In this judgment, I will refer to SGH and its subsidiary companies collectively as the “Smart Glove Group” or “SGG”.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 1 × Plaintiff’s Closing Submissions (“PCS”) at para 8.", "Foot Note 2 × PCS at para 9."]

rows.content_text: Within the Smart Glove Group, SGI is the sales and distribution arm. SGC, GX, PGI and Sigma (collectively, the “SGG Manufacturers”) are the companies which operate factories and/or plants manufacturing, inter alia , nitrile gloves. All gloves produced under the disputed agreements in this case were produced by the SGG Manufacturers. 3 Foot Note 3 × Affidavit of Evidence-in-Chief of Mr Ahmad Faizi Mohd Kamil (“Faizi’s AEIC”) at para 7.
rows.html_raw:
6 Within the Smart Glove Group, SGI is the sales and distribution arm. SGC, GX, PGI and Sigma (collectively, the “SGG Manufacturers”) are the companies which operate factories and/or plants manufacturing, inter alia, nitrile gloves. All gloves produced under the disputed agreements in this case were produced by the SGG Manufacturers.
Foot Note 3
 Affidavit of Evidence-in-Chief of Mr Ahmad Faizi Mohd Kamil (“Faizi’s AEIC”) at para 7.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 3 × Affidavit of Evidence-in-Chief of Mr Ahmad Faizi Mohd Kamil (“Faizi’s AEIC”) at para 7."]

rows.content_text: SGI called three factual witnesses to prove its case. These were Mr Long Say Sing (“Mr Long”), the General Manager of SGI, 4 Foot Note 4 × Affidavit of Evidence-in-Chief of Mr Long Say Sing (“Long’s AEIC”) at para 1. Mr Hew Yew Fook (“Mr Hew”), the Chief Operating Officer of SGH, 5 Foot Note 5 × Affidavit of Evidence-in-Chief of Mr Hew Yew Fook (“Hew’s AEIC”) at para 1. and Mr Ahmad Faizi Mohd Kamil (“Mr Faizi”), the Chief Corporate Officer of SGH. 6 Foot Note 6 × Faizi’s AEIC at para 1. SGI also called Mr Dawin Tang Keng Wai (“Mr Tang”), a director at PKF Covenant Equity Consulting Sdn Bhd and a Chartered Accountant, as its expert witness to quantify the parties’ claims and counterclaims. 7 Foot Note 7 × Affidavit of Evidence-in-Chief of Mr Dawin Tang Keng Wai (“Tang’s AEIC”) at para 1.

rows.content_text: Background to the dispute
rows.html_raw: Background to the dispute

rows.content_text: As alluded to at [1] above, the dispute centres on a supply agreement and three sets of purchase orders entered into between FSH and SGI, which I will now detail.
rows.html_raw: 10 As alluded to at [1] above, the dispute centres on a supply agreement and three sets of purchase orders entered into between FSH and SGI, which I will now detail.

rows.content_text: The Supply Agreement
rows.html_raw: The Supply Agreement

rows.content_text: In May 2020, FSH enquired with SGI on the supply of powder free nitrile gloves. 15 Foot Note 15 × Statement of Claim (Amendment No. 1) (“SOC-A1”) at para 3; Defence and Counterclaim (Amendment No. 3) (“D&C-A3”) at para 4. On 11 June 2020, following some discussions, SGI and FSH entered into a Memorandum of Agreement for the Supply of Medical Examination Gloves (the “Supply Agreement”). 16 Foot Note 16 × PCS at para 12; DCS at para 9. While the agreement was expressed to be between SGI and “Full Support Group”, the parties have always understood the agreement to be between SGI and FSH. 17 Foot Note 17 × SOC-A1 at para 6; D&C-A3 at para 7.
rows.html_raw:
11 In May 2020, FSH enquired with SGI on the supply of powder free nitrile gloves.
Foot Note 15
 Statement of Claim (Amendment No. 1) (“SOC-A1”) at para 3; Defence and Counterclaim (Amendment No. 3) (“D&C-A3”) at para 4.
On 11 June 2020, following some discussions, SGI and FSH entered into a Memorandum of Agreement for the Supply of Medical Examination Gloves (the “Supply Agreement”).
Foot Note 16
 PCS at para 12; DCS at para 9.
While the agreement was expressed to be between SGI and “Full Support Group”, the parties have always understood the agreement to be between SGI and FSH.
Foot Note 17
 SOC-A1 at para 6; D&C-A3 at para 7.

rows.content_text: The parties differ on the interpretation of various aspects of the Supply Agreement. However, the agreement effectively provides for the tentative sale and purchase of 1,300,000,000 Gen-X 3mil nitrile powder-free blue gloves (“3mil gloves”) as follows: 18 Foot Note 18 × Agreed Bundle of Documents (Volume 1 of 52) (“1AB”) 7–11.
rows.html_raw:
12 The parties differ on the interpretation of various aspects of the Supply Agreement. However, the agreement effectively provides for the tentative sale and purchase of 1,300,000,000 Gen-X 3mil nitrile powder-free blue gloves (“3mil gloves”) as follows:
Foot Note 18
 Agreed Bundle of Documents (Volume 1 of 52) (“1AB”) 7–11.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 18 × Agreed Bundle of Documents (Volume 1 of 52) (“1AB”) 7–11."]

rows.content_text: 1. SUPPLY OF PRODUCTS
rows.html_raw: 1. SUPPLY OF PRODUCTS

rows.content_text: 1.1 Purchaser intends to purchase one billion three hundred million (1,300,000,000) pieces of gloves, over a period of 7 months, from June 2020 to December 2020. The quantities and/or the period of deliveries may be increased and/or extended as may be agreed between the parties.
rows.html_raw: 1.1 Purchaser intends to purchase one billion three hundred million (1,300,000,000) pieces of gloves, over a period of 7 months, from June 2020 to December 2020. The quantities and/or the period of deliveries may be increased and/or extended as may be agreed between the parties.

rows.content_text: 1.2 The Supplier undertakes to use all reasonable endeavours to meet all orders for the Product required by the Purchaser in accordance with the agreed terms of delivery. The tentative schedule of deliveries, subject to receipt of advanced payment by Supplier as per clause 2.2, are as follows:-
rows.html_raw: 1.2 The Supplier undertakes to use all reasonable endeavours to meet all orders for the Product required by the Purchaser in accordance with the agreed terms of delivery. The tentative schedule of deliveries, subject to receipt of advanced payment by Supplier as per clause 2.2, are as follows:-

rows.content_text: (a) Approximately 100 million pieces (i.e. +/- 10%) in the month of June 2020,
rows.html_raw: (a) Approximately 100 million pieces (i.e. +/- 10%) in the month of June 2020,

rows.content_text: (b) Approximately 200 million pieces each month (i.e. +/- 10%) for the months of July 2020 to December 2020.
rows.html_raw: (b) Approximately 200 million pieces each month (i.e. +/- 10%) for the months of July 2020 to December 2020.

rows.content_text: The actual quantity to be delivered for each month will be dependent of the quantity that can be fitted into the shipping container, the vessel sailing dates or other similar factors.
rows.html_raw: The actual quantity to be delivered for each month will be dependent of the quantity that can be fitted into the shipping container, the vessel sailing dates or other similar factors.

rows.content_text: 1.3 The Product that Purchaser intends to purchase is 240mm 3mil Nitrile Powder-free Blue gloves (weight M: 3.50g +/- 0.2g), in the Supplier’s brand “ Gen-X ”.
rows.html_raw: 1.3 The Product that Purchaser intends to purchase is 240mm 3mil Nitrile Powder-free Blue gloves (weight M: 3.50g +/- 0.2g), in the Supplier’s brand “Gen-X”.

rows.content_text: 1.4 The Purchaser shall issue a Purchase Order (“PO”) for the order to be placed for the supply of gloves detailing all the requirements of the order including the Product specifications, quantity, quality, pricing, and time of delivery.
rows.html_raw: 1.4 The Purchaser shall issue a Purchase Order (“PO”) for the order to be placed for the supply of gloves detailing all the requirements of the order including the Product specifications, quantity, quality, pricing, and time of delivery.

rows.content_text: 1.5 Upon acceptance of the order(s), the Supplier shall issue a Proforma Invoice (PI) confirming the details of the order including the specifications, quantity, quality, pricing, and the estimated time of delivery(ies). Where required, separate PIs may be issued for each month of delivery.
rows.html_raw: 1.5 Upon acceptance of the order(s), the Supplier shall issue a Proforma Invoice (PI) confirming the details of the order including the specifications, quantity, quality, pricing, and the estimated time of delivery(ies). Where required, separate PIs may be issued for each month of delivery.
…

rows.content_text: 1.7 The Supplier shall ship the Product to the Purchaser’s designated location(s) in accordance with the following:
rows.html_raw: 1.7 The Supplier shall ship the Product to the Purchaser’s designated location(s) in accordance with the following:
…

rows.content_text: (b) The Purchaser shall enter into and bear all costs relating to the contract of carriage and contract of insurance.
rows.html_raw: (b) The Purchaser shall enter into and bear all costs relating to the contract of carriage and contract of insurance.
…

rows.content_text: As regards the prices and payment, the Supply Agreement provides for a default price of US$82.50 per 1,000 pieces of gloves, for a total contract sum of US$107,250,000:
rows.html_raw: 13 As regards the prices and payment, the Supply Agreement provides for a default price of US$82.50 per 1,000 pieces of gloves, for a total contract sum of US$107,250,000:

rows.content_text: 2. PRICES AND PAYMENT
rows.html_raw: 2. PRICES AND PAYMENT

rows.content_text: 2.1 For the quantity of 1.3 billion pieces under this MOA, Parties have agreed that the price shall be US Dollars Eighty-Two and Fifty Cents (US$82.50) per 1000 pieces of gloves, for a total contract sum of US Dollars One Hundred Seven Million, Two Hundred and Fifty Thousand only (US$107,250,000). The price shall be fixed for the full quantity unless there are extenuating circumstances beyond Supplier’s control that both parties agree warrants a change.
rows.html_raw: 2.1 For the quantity of 1.3 billion pieces under this MOA, Parties have agreed that the price shall be US Dollars Eighty-Two and Fifty Cents (US$82.50) per 1000 pieces of gloves, for a total contract sum of US Dollars One Hundred Seven Million, Two Hundred and Fifty Thousand only (US$107,250,000). The price shall be fixed for the full quantity unless there are extenuating circumstances beyond Supplier’s control that both parties agree warrants a change.

rows.content_text: 2.2 The payment term shall be as follows:-
rows.html_raw: 2.2 The payment term shall be as follows:-

rows.content_text:
A table with numbers and a few months

rows.content_text: For avoidance of doubt, the above delivery quantities and corresponding payments are subject to variation depending on factors mentioned in clause 1.2.
rows.html_raw: For avoidance of doubt, the above delivery quantities and corresponding payments are subject to variation depending on factors mentioned in clause 1.2.

rows.content_text: 2.3 The time for payment shall be of the essence and no payment shall be deemed to have been made until the Supplier has received payment in full in its bank account. The details of the bank account shall be notified in the PI.
rows.html_raw: 2.3 The time for payment shall be of the essence and no payment shall be deemed to have been made until the Supplier has received payment in full in its bank account. The details of the bank account shall be notified in the PI.

rows.content_text: 2.4 If Purchaser fails to make payment in accordance with the above schedule, Supplier reserves the right to withhold shipment or set-off any of the advance payment against any outstanding invoices.
rows.html_raw: 2.4 If Purchaser fails to make payment in accordance with the above schedule, Supplier reserves the right to withhold shipment or set-off any of the advance payment against any outstanding invoices.

rows.content_text: The Supply Agreement also contains certain warranties made by SGI regarding the quality of the gloves supplied:
rows.html_raw: 14 The Supply Agreement also contains certain warranties made by SGI regarding the quality of the gloves supplied:

rows.content_text: 3. SUPPLIER’S WARRANTY
rows.html_raw: 3. SUPPLIER’S WARRANTY

rows.content_text: 3.1 Supplier hereby warrants that all gloves are manufactured by SGH’s wholly owned subsidiaries in accordance with the prevailing quality standards as specified for each of the type of gloves. All gloves supplied by Supplier shall undergo quality inspection to conform to the quality standards for the gloves including any applicable standards as required by Purchaser.
rows.html_raw: 3.1 Supplier hereby warrants that all gloves are manufactured by SGH’s wholly owned subsidiaries in accordance with the prevailing quality standards as specified for each of the type of gloves. All gloves supplied by Supplier shall undergo quality inspection to conform to the quality standards for the gloves including any applicable standards as required by Purchaser.

rows.content_text: 3.2 Supplier verifies that its affiliates practices Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) and is certified to ISO 9001 and ISO 13485 and ensures that it is a well-managed manufacturing operation. Upon request by Purchaser, Supplier shall provide copies of its affiliated factories’ ISO certification, EN455 certification, EN374 PPE certification and MDR Certificate of Conformity.
rows.html_raw: 3.2 Supplier verifies that its affiliates practices Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) and is certified to ISO 9001 and ISO 13485 and ensures that it is a well-managed manufacturing operation. Upon request by Purchaser, Supplier shall provide copies of its affiliated factories’ ISO certification, EN455 certification, EN374 PPE certification and MDR Certificate of Conformity.

rows.content_text: Finally, the Supply Agreement contains clauses pertaining to force majeure, variation of terms and waiver:
rows.html_raw: 15 Finally, the Supply Agreement contains clauses pertaining to force majeure, variation of terms and waiver:

rows.content_text: 4.4 Force Majeure . Either Party shall be excused from any delay or failure in performance required hereunder if caused by reason of any occurrence or contingency beyond its reasonable control, including, but not limited to, acts of God, acts of war, fire, insurrection, strikes, lock-outs or other serious labour disputes, riots, earthquakes, floods, explosions or other acts of nature…
rows.html_raw: 4.4 Force Majeure. Either Party shall be excused from any delay or failure in performance required hereunder if caused by reason of any occurrence or contingency beyond its reasonable control, including, but not limited to, acts of God, acts of war, fire, insurrection, strikes, lock-outs or other serious labour disputes, riots, earthquakes, floods, explosions or other acts of nature…

rows.content_text: 4.5 Variation . This MOA may only be varied or amended upon mutual MOA of both Parties in writing.
rows.html_raw: 4.5 Variation. This MOA may only be varied or amended upon mutual MOA of both Parties in writing.

rows.content_text: 4.6 Waiver . No failure or delay by a party to exercise any right or remedy provided under this Agreement or by law shall constitute a waiver of that or any other right or remedy, nor shall it preclude or restrict the further exercise of that or any other right or remedy. No single or partial exercise of such right or remedy shall preclude or restrict the further exercise of that or any other right or remedy
rows.html_raw: 4.6 Waiver. No failure or delay by a party to exercise any right or remedy provided under this Agreement or by law shall constitute a waiver of that or any other right or remedy, nor shall it preclude or restrict the further exercise of that or any other right or remedy. No single or partial exercise of such right or remedy shall preclude or restrict the further exercise of that or any other right or remedy

rows.content_text: The first purchase order
rows.html_raw: The first purchase order

rows.content_text: After the Supply Agreement was concluded, FSH issued a purchase order to SGI dated 12 June 2020 for a total of 1,302,000,000 pieces of 3mil gloves (“PO1”), comprising 372 orders, as well as a summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Blue Nitrile PF Disposable Gloves”. 19 Foot Note 19 × 1AB 15–54; SOC-A1 at para 8 and D&C-A3 at para 13. This summary page contains tables reflecting a “Monthly Summary” and “Weekly Schedule” respectively. 20 Foot Note 20 × 1AB 54.

rows.content_text: On 16 June 2020, FSH issued a revised purchase order increasing the number of 3mil gloves to 1,306,500,000 pieces at US$82.50 per 1,000 gloves, for a total sum of US$107,786,250 (“PO1.2”), comprising 390 orders. FSH also issued a signed summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Blue Nitrile PF Disposable Gloves” (“PO1.2 Summary Page”). 21 Foot Note 21 × 1AB 57–101; SOC-A1 at para 9 and D&C-A3 at para 13. Again, this summary page contains tables reflecting a “Monthly Summary” and “Weekly Schedule” respectively. 22 Foot Note 22 × 1AB 101.

rows.content_text: 1. Delivery starting from June till December 2020.
rows.html_raw: 1. Delivery starting from June till December 2020.

rows.content_text: 2. Payment term is 80% down-payment upon PI confirmation, 20% before shipment booking for 1st 3 months and subsequently monthly.
rows.html_raw: 2. Payment term is 80% down-payment upon PI confirmation, 20% before shipment booking for 1st 3 months and subsequently monthly.

rows.content_text: 3. Customer will need to pay 80% down-payment from June / July / August upon PI confirmation, and 20% upon delivery done.
rows.html_raw: 3. Customer will need to pay 80% down-payment from June / July / August upon PI confirmation, and 20% upon delivery done.

rows.content_text: 4. Then in July, customer will pay 20% for June shipment and need to place order for September, make down-payment 80% (September PO) and subsequently follow on by monthly.
rows.html_raw: 4. Then in July, customer will pay 20% for June shipment and need to place order for September, make down-payment 80% (September PO) and subsequently follow on by monthly.
…

rows.content_text: The 1st PI also stipulates that in the event of late payment by FSH, “interest at the rate of 1.5% per month is chargeable for any invoiced amount not paid in accordance with the agreed payment terms from its due date to the date of payment …”. 26 Foot Note 26 × 1AB 56.
rows.html_raw:
21 The 1st PI also stipulates that in the event of late payment by FSH, “interest at the rate of 1.5% per month is chargeable for any invoiced amount not paid in accordance with the agreed payment terms from its due date to the date of payment …”.
Foot Note 26
 1AB 56.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 26 × 1AB 56."]

rows.content_text: On 17 June 2020, pursuant to PO1.2 and the 1st PI, FSH transferred US$33,165,000 to SGI. 27 Foot Note 27 × SOC-A1 at para 12 and D&C-A3 at para 18.
rows.html_raw:
22 On 17 June 2020, pursuant to PO1.2 and the 1st PI, FSH transferred US$33,165,000 to SGI.
Foot Note 27
 SOC-A1 at para 12 and D&C-A3 at para 18.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 27 × SOC-A1 at para 12 and D&C-A3 at para 18."]

rows.content_text: On 29 June 2020, SGI issued a revised Proforma Invoice (“Revised 1st PI”). The terms in the Revised 1st PI remain largely similar to those in the 1st PI, save that some revisions were made to the schedule of payments. 28 Foot Note 28 × See 1AB 102–103 cf 1AB 55 ; PCS at para 18.
rows.html_raw:
23 On 29 June 2020, SGI issued a revised Proforma Invoice (“Revised 1st PI”). The terms in the Revised 1st PI remain largely similar to those in the 1st PI, save that some revisions were made to the schedule of payments.
Foot Note 28
 See 1AB 102–103 cf 1AB 55; PCS at para 18.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 28 × See 1AB 102–103 cf 1AB 55 ; PCS at para 18."]

rows.content_text: Subsequently, on 21 August 2020, FSH issued a further revised purchase order revising the size mix per container for the 1,306,500,000 pieces of 3mil gloves (“PO1.3”), comprising 390 orders, as well as a summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Blue Nitrile 3Mil PF Disposable Gloves” (“PO1.3 Summary Page”). 29 Foot Note 29 × 1AB 129–175; SOC-A1 at para 14 and D&C-A3 at para 18. FSH requested for the change of size mix earlier the same month. 30 Foot Note 30 × DCS at para 101.
rows.html_raw:
24 Subsequently, on 21 August 2020, FSH issued a further revised purchase order revising the size mix per container for the 1,306,500,000 pieces of 3mil gloves (“PO1.3”), comprising 390 orders, as well as a summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Blue Nitrile 3Mil PF Disposable Gloves” (“PO1.3 Summary Page”).
Foot Note 29
 1AB 129–175; SOC-A1 at para 14 and D&C-A3 at para 18.
FSH requested for the change of size mix earlier the same month.
Foot Note 30
 DCS at para 101.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 29 × 1AB 129–175; SOC-A1 at para 14 and D&C-A3 at para 18.", "Foot Note 30 × DCS at para 101."]

rows.content_text: The PO1.3 Summary Page contains at least three material differences from the summary pages in the previous two purchase orders. First, the revised size mix for certain containers were stipulated. 31 Foot Note 31 × 1AB 175. More specifically, whereas the PO1.2 Summary Page provided that each container was to contain 670 cases of small-sized gloves, 1,675 cases of medium-sized gloves and 1,005 cases of large-sized gloves, the PO1.3 Summary Page provided that for 297 out of the total 390 orders, the containers were to contain 100 cases of small-sized gloves, 1,400 cases of medium-sized gloves, 1,200 cases of large-sized gloves, and 650 cases of extra-large-sized gloves. 32 Foot Note 32 × 1AB 175.
rows.html_raw:
25 The PO1.3 Summary Page contains at least three material differences from the summary pages in the previous two purchase orders. First, the revised size mix for certain containers were stipulated.
Foot Note 31
 1AB 175.
More specifically, whereas the PO1.2 Summary Page provided that each container was to contain 670 cases of small-sized gloves, 1,675 cases of medium-sized gloves and 1,005 cases of large-sized gloves, the PO1.3 Summary Page provided that for 297 out of the total 390 orders, the containers were to contain 100 cases of small-sized gloves, 1,400 cases of medium-sized gloves, 1,200 cases of large-sized gloves, and 650 cases of extra-large-sized gloves.
Foot Note 32
 1AB 175.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 31 × 1AB 175.", "Foot Note 32 × 1AB 175."]

rows.content_text: Second, the table containing the “Monthly Summary” (see [18] above) became relabelled as the “Orginal [sic] Monthly Summary”, though its contents remained the same. 33 Foot Note 33 × 1AB 175 cf 1AB 101.
rows.html_raw:
26 Second, the table containing the “Monthly Summary” (see [18] above) became relabelled as the “Orginal [sic] Monthly Summary”, though its contents remained the same.
Foot Note 33
 1AB 175 cf 1AB 101.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 33 × 1AB 175 cf 1AB 101."]

rows.content_text: Third, the table which was originally inserted under the “Weekly Schedule” was struck out. In its place, the following line was inserted:
rows.html_raw: 27 Third, the table which was originally inserted under the “Weekly Schedule” was struck out. In its place, the following line was inserted:

rows.content_text: Weekly Schedule: Please see email from Yeoh for agreed weekly production plan.
rows.html_raw: Weekly Schedule: Please see email from Yeoh for agreed weekly production plan.

rows.content_text: The reference to “Yeoh” is to Mr Yeoh Yao Zhi (“Mr Yeoh”), a Customer Service Executive from SGI. 34 Foot Note 34 × DCS at para 90(e).
rows.html_raw:
The reference to “Yeoh” is to Mr Yeoh Yao Zhi (“Mr Yeoh”), a Customer Service Executive from SGI.
Foot Note 34
 DCS at para 90(e).
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 34 × DCS at para 90(e)."]

rows.content_text:
From this juncture, I will refer to PO1, PO1.2 and PO1.3 collectively as the “1st PO”. Between June 2020 and April 2021, several transactions took place under the 1st PO. The table below summarises these transactions. The parties agree to proceed on the contents stated in the table. However, the characterisation of the 80% paid upfront by FSH for each tranche of orders is disputed. While SGI contends that these are forfeitable deposits, FSH argues that these are refundable advance payments. 35 Foot Note 35 × PCS at paras 23 and 24; Zheng’s AEIC at paras 16–22.

---table---
Order Numbers | Delivery status | 80% already paid by FSH | 20% balance | Total amount
FSH16782 to FSH16942 and FSH17153 (162 orders) | 542,700,000 of 542,700,000 gloves delivered | Paid in full | Paid in full | US$46,923,030 paid
FSH16943 to FSH16962 (20 orders) | 67,000,000 of 67,000,000 gloves delivered | Paid in full | Not paid | US$4,422,000 paid; US$2,096,524 unpaid
FSH16963 to FSH17171 (208 orders) | 696,800,000 of 696,800,000 gloves undelivered | Paid in full | Not paid | US$45,988,800 paid
---end-table---
rows.html_raw:
28 From this juncture, I will refer to PO1, PO1.2 and PO1.3 collectively as the “1st PO”. Between June 2020 and April 2021, several transactions took place under the 1st PO. The table below summarises these transactions. The parties agree to proceed on the contents stated in the table. However, the characterisation of the 80% paid upfront by FSH for each tranche of orders is disputed. While SGI contends that these are forfeitable deposits, FSH argues that these are refundable advance payments.
Foot Note 35
 PCS at paras 23 and 24; Zheng’s AEIC at paras 16–22.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 35 × PCS at paras 23 and 24; Zheng’s AEIC at paras 16–22."]

rows.content_text: Ultimately, SGI did not adhere to the delivery schedules set out in the PO1.3 Summary Page, although the parties are in dispute as to whether SGI was obligated to do so.
rows.html_raw: 29 Ultimately, SGI did not adhere to the delivery schedules set out in the PO1.3 Summary Page, although the parties are in dispute as to whether SGI was obligated to do so.

rows.content_text: The 5mil purchase order
rows.html_raw: The 5mil purchase order

rows.content_text: In July 2020, SGI and FSH further discussed the supply of 5 mil Nitrile Dark Smart Blue gloves to FSH. 36 Foot Note 36 × SOC-A1 at para 30; D&C-A3 at para 33.
rows.html_raw:
30  In July 2020, SGI and FSH further discussed the supply of 5 mil Nitrile Dark Smart Blue gloves to FSH.
Foot Note 36
 SOC-A1 at para 30; D&C-A3 at para 33.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 36 × SOC-A1 at para 30; D&C-A3 at para 33."]

rows.content_text: On 4 August 2020, FSH issued a purchase order to SGI for a total of 346,590,000,000 pieces of “Gen-X 5mil Dark Smart Blue Regular Cuff & One order for Long Cuff” gloves (“5mil gloves”), comprising 141 orders for regular cuff gloves (at US$86 per 1,000 gloves) and one order for long cuff gloves (at US$110 per 1,000 gloves), for a total sum of US$29,867,940. 37 Foot Note 37 × SOC-A1 at para 31; D&C-A3 at paras 33–34; 1AB 112 and 113. FSH also issued a signed summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Gen-X 5mil Dark Smart Blue Regular Cuff & One order for Long Cuff”. This summary page contains tables respectively reflecting a “Monthly Summary” and a “Weekly Schedule Regular Cuff”. 38 Foot Note 38 × 1AB 104–113.
rows.html_raw:
31 On 4 August 2020, FSH issued a purchase order to SGI for a total of 346,590,000,000 pieces of “Gen-X 5mil Dark Smart Blue Regular Cuff & One order for Long Cuff” gloves (“5mil gloves”), comprising 141 orders for regular cuff gloves (at US$86 per 1,000 gloves) and one order for long cuff gloves (at US$110 per 1,000 gloves), for a total sum of US$29,867,940.
Foot Note 37
 SOC-A1 at para 31; D&C-A3 at paras 33–34; 1AB 112 and 113.
FSH also issued a signed summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Gen-X 5mil Dark Smart Blue Regular Cuff & One order for Long Cuff”. This summary page contains tables respectively reflecting a “Monthly Summary” and a “Weekly Schedule Regular Cuff”.
Foot Note 38
 1AB 104–113.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 37 × SOC-A1 at para 31; D&C-A3 at paras 33–34; 1AB 112 and 113.", "Foot Note 38 × 1AB 104–113."]

rows.content_text: On 6 August 2020, FSH issued a revised purchase order for the 5mil gloves (the “5mil PO”), along with a signed summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Gen-X 5mil Dark Smart Blue Regular Cuff & One order for Long Cuff- now with updated Exfactory dates” (the “5mil PO Summary Page”). 39 Foot Note 39 × SOC-A1 at para 32; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 114–123. The two purchase orders differ in the details under the tables respectively reflecting the “Monthly Summary” and “Weekly Schedule Regular Cuff”. 40 Foot Note 40 × 1AB 113 cf 1AB 12 3.
rows.html_raw:
32 On 6 August 2020, FSH issued a revised purchase order for the 5mil gloves (the “5mil PO”), along with a signed summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Gen-X 5mil Dark Smart Blue Regular Cuff & One order for Long Cuff- now with updated Exfactory dates” (the “5mil PO Summary Page”).
Foot Note 39
 SOC-A1 at para 32; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 114–123.
The two purchase orders differ in the details under the tables respectively reflecting the “Monthly Summary” and “Weekly Schedule Regular Cuff”.
Foot Note 40
 1AB 113 cf 1AB 123.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 39 × SOC-A1 at para 32; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 114–123.", "Foot Note 40 × 1AB 113 cf 1AB 12 3."]

rows.content_text: Under the 5mil PO Summary Page, the “Monthly Summary” is reflected as such:
rows.html_raw: 33 Under the 5mil PO Summary Page, the “Monthly Summary” is reflected as such:

rows.content_text: On 10 August 2020, SGI accepted the 5mil PO by issuing two proforma invoices for: 41 Foot Note 41 × SOC-A1 at para 33; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 124 and 127.
rows.html_raw:
34 On 10 August 2020, SGI accepted the 5mil PO by issuing two proforma invoices for:
Foot Note 41
 SOC-A1 at para 33; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 124 and 127.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 41 × SOC-A1 at para 33; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 124 and 127."]

rows.content_text: (a) 344,040,000 pieces of 5mil gloves (regular cuff) for US$29,587,440 (the “1st 5mil PI”); and
rows.html_raw: (a) 344,040,000 pieces of 5mil gloves (regular cuff) for US$29,587,440 (the “1st 5mil PI”); and

rows.content_text: (b) 2,550,000 pieces of 5mil gloves (long cuff) for US$280,500 (the “2nd 5mil PI”).
rows.html_raw: (b) 2,550,000 pieces of 5mil gloves (long cuff) for US$280,500 (the “2nd 5mil PI”).

rows.content_text: On 5 October 2020, SGI issued a revised Proforma Invoice (the “Revised 1st 5mil PI”), under which the prices of the 5mil gloves (regular cuff) were revised so that the total sum is US$31,036,800. 42 Foot Note 42 × SOC-A1 at para 34; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 176–177 cf 1AB 126–127.
rows.html_raw:
35  On 5 October 2020, SGI issued a revised Proforma Invoice (the “Revised 1st 5mil PI”), under which the prices of the 5mil gloves (regular cuff) were revised so that the total sum is US$31,036,800.
Foot Note 42
 SOC-A1 at para 34; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 176–177 cf 1AB 126–127.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 42 × SOC-A1 at para 34; D&C-A3 at para 33; 1AB 176–177 cf 1AB 126–127."]

rows.content_text: In the 1st 5mil PI, 2nd 5mil PI, and Revised 1st 5mil PI (collectively, the “5mil PIs”), the following “Remarks/Special Instruction[s]” are stated: 43 Foot Note 43 × 1AB 125, 127 and 177.
rows.html_raw:
36 In the 1st 5mil PI, 2nd 5mil PI, and Revised 1st 5mil PI (collectively, the “5mil PIs”), the following “Remarks/Special Instruction[s]” are stated:
Foot Note 43
 1AB 125, 127 and 177.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 43 × 1AB 125, 127 and 177."]

rows.html_raw: …

rows.content_text: 2. Payment term is 80% down-payment upon PI confirmation, 20% before shipment booking for 1st 3 months and subsequently monthly.
rows.html_raw: 2. Payment term is 80% down-payment upon PI confirmation, 20% before shipment booking for 1st 3 months and subsequently monthly.

rows.content_text: 3. Customer will need to pay 80% down-payment from Aug / Sep / Oct upon PI confirmation, and 20% upon delivery done.
rows.html_raw: 3. Customer will need to pay 80% down-payment from Aug / Sep / Oct upon PI confirmation, and 20% upon delivery done.

rows.content_text: 4. Then in September, customer will pay 20% for August shipment and need to place order for November, make down-payment 80% (November PO) and subsequently follow on by monthly.
rows.html_raw: 4. Then in September, customer will pay 20% for August shipment and need to place order for November, make down-payment 80% (November PO) and subsequently follow on by monthly.
…

rows.content_text: PLEASE NOTE THAT PRICE STATED IN THIS PROFORMA INVOICE IS SUBJECT TO CHANGE AND THE FINAL PRICE WILL BE NOTIFIED TO THE BUYER PRIOR TO THE CONFIRMED DATE OF DELIVERY.
rows.html_raw: PLEASE NOTE THAT PRICE STATED IN THIS PROFORMA INVOICE IS SUBJECT TO CHANGE AND THE FINAL PRICE WILL BE NOTIFIED TO THE BUYER PRIOR TO THE CONFIRMED DATE OF DELIVERY.

rows.content_text: All three Proforma Invoices also contain a late payment interest clause similar to that found in the 1st PI (see [21] above).
rows.html_raw: 37 All three Proforma Invoices also contain a late payment interest clause similar to that found in the 1st PI (see [21] above).

rows.content_text:
Between September 2020 and April 2021, several transactions took place under the 5mil PO. The table below summarises these transactions. Again, parties agree to proceed on the contents stated in the table, and are only disputing the characterisation of the 80% of monies already paid by FSH. 44 Foot Note 44 × PCS at paras 32 and 33; Zheng’s AEIC at paras 34–37.

---table---
Order Numbers | Delivery status | 80% already paid by FSH | 20% balance | Total amount
FSH17219 to FSH17348 (130 orders) | 2,550,000 of 2,550,000 long cuff and 314,760,000 of 314,760,000 regular cuff gloves delivered | Paid in full | Paid in full | US$30,307,990.80 paid
FSH17349 to FSH17360 (12 orders) | 29,280,000 of 29,280,000 regular cuff gloves delivered | Paid in full | Not paid | US$2,014,464 paid; US$1,406,828.80 unpaid
---end-table---
rows.html_raw:
38 Between September 2020 and April 2021, several transactions took place under the 5mil PO. The table below summarises these transactions. Again, parties agree to proceed on the contents stated in the table, and are only disputing the characterisation of the 80% of monies already paid by FSH.
Foot Note 44
 PCS at paras 32 and 33; Zheng’s AEIC at paras 34–37.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 44 × PCS at paras 32 and 33; Zheng’s AEIC at paras 34–37."]

rows.content_text: The second purchase order
rows.html_raw: The second purchase order

rows.content_text: Between June and November 2020, while the 1st PO and the 5mil PO were being performed, SGI and FSH discussed the further supply of 3mil gloves for 2021. 45 Foot Note 45 × SOC-A1 at para 20; D&C-A3 at para 25. In November 2020, FSH informed SGI that it was looking to order at least 50 containers of gloves per month, in addition to the original orders under the 1st PO. 46 Foot Note 46 × SOC-A1 at para 21; D&C-A3 at para 25.
rows.html_raw:
39 Between June and November 2020, while the 1st PO and the 5mil PO were being performed, SGI and FSH discussed the further supply of 3mil gloves for 2021.
Foot Note 45
 SOC-A1 at para 20; D&C-A3 at para 25.
In November 2020, FSH informed SGI that it was looking to order at least 50 containers of gloves per month, in addition to the original orders under the 1st PO.
Foot Note 46
 SOC-A1 at para 21; D&C-A3 at para 25.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 45 × SOC-A1 at para 20; D&C-A3 at para 25.", "Foot Note 46 × SOC-A1 at para 21; D&C-A3 at para 25."]

rows.content_text: On 19 November 2020, FSH therefore issued a purchase order for an additional 469,000,000 pieces of 3mil gloves at US$93.50 per 1,000 gloves for a total of US$43,851,500 (the “2nd PO”), comprising 140 orders. FSH also issued a signed summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Gen-X 3mil Nitrile Baby Blue” (the “2nd PO Summary Page”). 47 Foot Note 47 × SOC-A1 at para 22; D&C-A3 at para 25; 1AB 181–190.
rows.html_raw:
40 On 19 November 2020, FSH therefore issued a purchase order for an additional 469,000,000 pieces of 3mil gloves at US$93.50 per 1,000 gloves for a total of US$43,851,500 (the “2nd PO”), comprising 140 orders. FSH also issued a signed summary page headed “Summary of Orders for Gen-X 3mil Nitrile Baby Blue” (the “2nd PO Summary Page”).
Foot Note 47
 SOC-A1 at para 22; D&C-A3 at para 25; 1AB 181–190.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 47 × SOC-A1 at para 22; D&C-A3 at para 25; 1AB 181–190."]

rows.content_text: In the 2nd PO, unlike in the 1st PO and the 5mil PO, the column titled “Estimated Ex-Factory Date From Smart” was left blank. 48 Foot Note 48 × PCS at para 36; see, eg , 1AB 189 .
rows.html_raw:
41 In the 2nd PO, unlike in the 1st PO and the 5mil PO, the column titled “Estimated Ex-Factory Date From Smart” was left blank.
Foot Note 48
 PCS at para 36; see, eg, 1AB 189.
rows.footnote_text: ["Foot Note 48 × PCS at para 36; see, eg , 1AB 189 ."]

rows.content_text: Moreover, there was no “Weekly Schedule” table reflected in the 2nd PO Summary Page. 49 Foot Note 49 × PCS at para 36; 1AB 190. There was, however, still a “Monthly Schedule” table:
rows.html_raw:
42 Moreover, there was no “Weekly Schedule” table reflected in the 2nd PO Summary Page.
Foot Note 49
 PCS at para 36; 1AB 190.
There was, however, still a “Monthly Schedule” table:

query.sql: select id, judgment_id, ordinal, paragraph_number, class_name, section_heading, content_text, html_raw, footnote_text, has_footnotes, has_table, has_figure, figure_src, figure_descriptions from judgments_fragments where "judgment_id" = :p0 order by ordinal limit 101
